engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Dias Wagon Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Dias Wagon in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru Dias Wagon EN07E engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Crank angle sensor failure - documented recall issue causing stalling and non-starting on 1999-2009 units.
Head gasket failure - common on high-mileage Kei engines, typically 70k-100k miles.

cards.altText: Subaru Dias Wagon EN07Z engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Supercharger wear - boost loss and oil consumption common after 60k miles on performance variants.
Crank angle sensor failure - affects all EN07 variants, including supercharged RS models.
Head gasket failure - turbo heat cycles accelerate gasket degradation on high-output units.

cards.altText: Subaru Dias Wagon EN07C engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carburettor wear - fuel delivery issues common on pre-2001 units with high mileage.
Head gasket failure - documented across all EN07 applications, including early carburettor models.
Crank angle sensor failure - recall affects all EN07 variants regardless of fuel system.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Dias Wagon Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Dias Wagon's EN07-series Kei engines are compact and efficient but share documented failure points with other JDM microvans. Crank angle sensor failure dominates replacement enquiries due to a known recall, followed by head gasket issues on high-mileage units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Crank Angle Sensor Failure - Why Subaru Dias Wagon EN07 Engines Stall Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Dias Wagon Classic, Classic Premium, RS, Base (1999-2009, all EN07 codes)
problems.rootCause: Original crank angle sensors degrade under heat and vibration, causing intermittent stalling, rough idle, and complete non-starting conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crank angle sensor with updated part, reset ECU, test drive
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before engine damage; 40-60k mile expectation on sensor

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EN07 engine with updated sensors, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month unlimited warranty, addresses root cause and other wear items

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru Dias Wagon worth £2,000-£6,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better value than repeated sensor replacements if the engine has high mileage or other wear issues. We recommend rebuilt units with updated sensors to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07 engine prices for your Subaru Dias Wagon

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Subaru Dias Wagon EN07 Coolant/Oil Cross-Contamination
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Dias Wagon Classic, Classic Premium, RS, Base (1999-2008, all EN07 codes)
problems.rootCause: Original head gaskets degrade under heat cycles and Kei engine vibration, allowing coolant and oil to mix, causing overheating and potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket with upgraded MLS unit, reseal, test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/heads undamaged; 30-50k mile expectation on gasket

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EN07 engine with MLS gaskets, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses head gasket + common EN07 failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Dias Wagon has high mileage or shows signs of coolant/oil mixing beyond the head gasket, a full rebuilt EN07 engine typically costs less than separate gasket repair + ancillary work. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded MLS gaskets.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07 engine prices for your Subaru Dias Wagon

problems.h4: Supercharger Wear - EN07Z Dias Wagon RS Boost Loss & Oil Consumption
problems.rootCause: Supercharger bearings and seals wear under boost pressure and heat, causing oil leakage into intake/exhaust and loss of boost performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, rebuild or replace with reconditioned unit, replace lines/gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block healthy; 40-60k mile expectation on supercharger

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EN07Z Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EN07Z with new/reconditioned supercharger, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses supercharger + common EN07 failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Dias Wagon RS has high mileage or shows signs of oil consumption beyond the supercharger, a full rebuilt EN07Z engine often costs less than separate supercharger + head gasket repairs. We recommend rebuilt units with supercharger health guarantees.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07Z supercharged engine prices for your Subaru Dias Wagon

problems.h4: Carburettor Wear - Early EN07C Dias Wagon Fuel Delivery Issues
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Dias Wagon Classic (early), Base carburettor (1999-2001, EN07C)
problems.rootCause: Carburettor jets and diaphragms wear on ageing mechanical fuel systems, causing rough idle, hesitation, and poor fuel economy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove carburettor, rebuild with new jets/diaphragms or replace with reconditioned unit
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block healthy; 40-60k mile expectation on carburettor

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EN07C engine with new/reconditioned carburettor, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses carburettor + common EN07 failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your early Dias Wagon has high mileage or shows signs of fuel delivery issues beyond the carburettor, a full rebuilt EN07C engine typically offers better value than a carburettor-only repair, especially on grey import vehicles where parts sourcing is complex.
problems.cta: Get quotes for low-fuel-consumption rebuilt Dias Wagon engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Dias Wagon
finalCta.paragraph: Crank angle sensor, head gasket, supercharger, or carburettor issues don't have to mean the end for your JDM Dias Wagon. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru Dias Wagon specialists who understand EN07 Kei engine compatibility with Sambar donors. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Dias Wagon Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Dias Wagon and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Subaru Dias Wagon Petrol Engines
items.description: Every Subaru Dias Wagon sold in the UK grey import market used a 0.66L petrol Kei engine, with carburettor EN07C for early models, fuel-injected EN07E for mainstream variants, and supercharged EN07Z for performance RS trims.
items.descriptor: Every Subaru Dias Wagon sold in the UK grey import market used a 0.66L petrol Kei engine, with carburettor EN07C for early models, fuel-injected EN07E for mainstream variants, and supercharged EN07Z for performance RS trims.
items.familiesLabel: Common Subaru Dias Wagon Petrol Engine Families
families: EN07C - Early Classic model carburettor Kei engine, most common on pre-2001 grey import replacements
EN07E - Mainstream fuel-injected Kei engine, minor refinements over EN07C for improved reliability and emissions
EN07Z - Supercharged performance Kei engine, high-demand for enthusiast and RS variant Dias Wagon owners
foundIn: First Generation (1999-2001) - EN07C carburettor naturally aspirated
Mainstream Production (2001-2008) - EN07E fuel injection naturally aspirated
knownFor: Compact SOHC Kei design when crank sensors are maintained
EN07Z supercharged delivers strong performance but requires vigilant servicing
Compatibility with Sambar EN07 donor engines simplifies sourcing for UK grey imports

engineYears.h2: Subaru Dias Wagon Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Dias Wagon's 1999-2008 Subaru-built production run, organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their EN07 engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices.

years.preview: Early Dias Wagon: EN07C Carburettor Debut
years.description: The launch years introduced the EN07C carburettor Kei engine to UK grey import Dias Wagon Classic models, establishing the compact but crank-sensor-sensitive platform.
keyChanges: Subaru Dias Wagon launched for JDM market; UK availability via grey import specialists
EN07C 0.66L SOHC carburettor Kei engine fitted to Classic and Base trims
Crank angle sensor recall campaign issued for 1999-2001 production units
2WD and 4WD drivetrain options with different ECU mapping
mainEngines: EN07C - 0.66L petrol carburettor naturally aspirated (Classic, Base)
knownFor: Economical carburettor Kei performance for UK grey import buyers
Crank sensor vigilance essential from 50k miles onwards
Strong compatibility with Sambar EN07 donor engines for replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2001 Subaru Dias Wagon replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru Dias Wagon crank sensor replacement • EN07C engine supply UK • Dias Wagon carburettor compatibility
enquiries: Subaru Dias Wagon crank sensor replacement

years.preview: Mainstream Dias Wagon: EN07E EGI Introduction
years.description: The mainstream years brought the fuel-injected EN07E Kei engine to UK grey import Dias Wagon Classic Premium and Base models, improving reliability and emissions compliance.
keyChanges: EN07E 0.66L SOHC fuel-injected Kei engine introduced across all mainstream trims
Minor ECU and fuel system refinements for improved driveability and emissions
Classic Premium trim introduced with enhanced interior and styling packages
Final Subaru-built production year 2008; low-mileage examples now sought after
mainEngines: EN07E - 0.66L petrol fuel-injected naturally aspirated (Classic, Classic Premium, Base)
knownFor: EN07E refinements improved reliability over early EN07C carburettor units
Classic Premium offered enhanced styling and equipment for enthusiast buyers
Low-mileage 2006-2008 examples command premium in UK grey import market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2008 Subaru Dias Wagon replacement engines
years.ticker: Dias Wagon EN07E fuel injection • Classic Premium engine compatibility • 2008 Dias Wagon low mileage engine

years.preview: Performance Dias Wagon RS: EN07Z Supercharged Introduction
years.description: The performance years brought the supercharged EN07Z Kei engine to UK grey import Dias Wagon RS models, raising replacement demand among enthusiast owners.
keyChanges: EN07Z 0.66L supercharged Kei engine introduced for RS performance trim
Increased output delivers 58hp with improved low-end torque for Kei class
Supercharger and fuel system refinements for performance reliability
RS models feature enhanced suspension, styling and interior upgrades
mainEngines: EN07Z - 0.66L petrol supercharged (RS, RS S321N chassis)
knownFor: EN07Z delivers maximum Kei performance with 58hp for enthusiast and off-road owners
Supercharged models require vigilant servicing to avoid boost/oil consumption issues
Low-mileage RS examples command premium in UK grey import enthusiast market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2008 Subaru Dias Wagon RS replacement engines
years.ticker: Dias Wagon EN07Z supercharger replacement • RS model engine compatibility • 2008 Dias Wagon RS low mileage

faq.h2: Subaru Dias Wagon Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Dias Wagon engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Dias Wagon engine replacements typically range from £800 for used EN07 units to £2,500 for rebuilt supercharged EN07Z engines, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£700 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Naturally aspirated EN07C/EN07E rebuilt: £1,200-£2,200 supply only
Supercharged EN07Z rebuilt: £1,400-£2,500 supply only
Supply & fit service adds £400-£700 to engine price
All quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get your free Subaru Dias Wagon engine replacement quote today

items.question: Which engine code fits my Subaru Dias Wagon?
items.answer: Your Dias Wagon uses either EN07C (carburettor, 1999-2001), EN07E (fuel injection, 2001-2008) or EN07Z (supercharged, 2003-2008) depending on trim and year. Enter your registration or VIN above to instantly verify your exact engine code.
keyPoints: Classic/Base models (1999-2001): EN07C carburettor naturally aspirated
Classic Premium/Base (2001-2008): EN07E fuel injection naturally aspirated
RS models (2003-2008): EN07Z supercharged
DVLA lookup confirms exact code before ordering replacement
items.cta: Verify your Dias Wagon engine code with our free compatibility checker

items.question: What is the difference between used and reconditioned Dias Wagon engines?
items.answer: Used engines are tested second-hand units from breakers, while reconditioned engines are fully rebuilt with new bearings, seals and upgraded components. Reconditioned units include longer warranties and address common failure points.
keyPoints: Used: £800-£1,500, 6-month warranty typical, mileage varies
Reconditioned: £1,200-£2,500, 12-month unlimited warranty standard
Reconditioned includes upgraded sensors and head gaskets
Reconditioned recommended for high-mileage or failure-damaged units
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru Dias Wagon engine options

items.answer: Most Subaru Dias Wagon engines are dispatched next-day from UK stock; fitting takes 1-2 days at approved Subaru specialists. Total turnaround from quote to road-ready is typically 3-5 working days.

Specialist fitting: 1-2 days at approved Subaru garage
Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours

items.question: Can I fit a different EN07 engine code in my Dias Wagon?
items.answer: EN07C and EN07E are often interchangeable with ECU verification, but EN07Z supercharged requires matching wiring and mounts. Use our compatibility checker or speak to a Subaru Dias Wagon specialist before ordering a different code.
keyPoints: EN07C and EN07E often interchangeable on naturally aspirated models
EN07Z requires matching ECU, wiring harness and fuel system
Mounting points and 4WD compatibility must be confirmed
Specialist advice recommended for cross-code swaps on grey imports
